name: Ventilator-associated pneumonia
description: Ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) — pneumonia developing during mechanical ventilation.

en.name: Ventilator-associated pneumonia
en.shortDescription: Ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) — pneumonia developing during mechanical ventilation.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) is pneumonia that develops in a patient who has been mechanically ventilated (typically beyond 48 hours of intubation). It is a key hospitalisation complication in intensive care, associated with prolonged ventilation, longer ICU stay and increased mortality, and is widely tracked as a quality indicator.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *259992 / 429271009 — Ventilator associated pneumonia*. It captures the VAP diagnosis specifically.

## Mapping Notes

- VAP is distinct from community-acquired or non-ventilator hospital-acquired pneumonia; map only pneumonia identified as ventilator-associated.
